assessment_items.question_content: **Carvaggio created three paintings about   [[☃ dropdown 1]] for the Contarelli Chapel in [[☃ dropdown 2]].**
hints:
The Contarelli Chapel in Rome is the location of three major works by Caravaggio on the subject of St. Matthew.  

**[Watch this video to review.](/humanities/monarchy-enlightenment/baroque-art1/baroque-italy/v/caravaggio-s-calling-of-st-matthew-c-1599-1600)**

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which figure in this painting is St. Matthew? **


![Painting showing Christ, at right with a halo, pointing toward a group of men at a table in a dark tavern-like setting, with a beam of light highlighting the surprised expressions of the figures.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/0511ee5fb510f75ae37b95b6ac08aa28.jpg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Christ points his head to Matthew, who looks up in surprise and points toward himself in disbelief.   

**[Watch this video to review.](/humanities/monarchy-enlightenment/baroque-art1/baroque-italy/v/caravaggio-s-calling-of-st-matthew-c-1599-1600)**

assessment_items.question_content:
**Art historians find it significant that Caravaggio based Christ's hand on which figure in Michelangelo's *Sistine Ceiling*?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Neither Peter nor Christ appears in Michelangelo's *Sistine Ceiling.*

By modeling Christ's hand on Michelangelo's hand of Adam, Caravaggio suggests that Matthew is recreated in this moment of redemption.  

**[Watch this video to review.](/humanities/monarchy-enlightenment/baroque-art1/baroque-italy/v/caravaggio-s-calling-of-st-matthew-c-1599-1600)**

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which best explains the setting in *The Calling of St. Matthew*?**

![Painting showing Christ, at right with a halo, pointing toward a group of men at a table in a dark tavern-like setting, with a beam of light highlighting the surprised expressions of the figures.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/e2f72f2355b8c6682ac4a140881ef55a.jpg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The painting contrasts strongly with the ornate marble of the chapel's architecture.

Caravaggio's dark and seedy room suggest a common environment that would have seemed familiar to viewers at the time.  It emphasizes the worldly setting where Matthew's spiritual revelation occurs.  

**[Watch this video to review.](/humanities/monarchy-enlightenment/baroque-art1/baroque-italy/v/caravaggio-s-calling-of-st-matthew-c-1599-1600)**

choices.content: Caravaggio wanted the scene set in a common and seedy environment.
Caravaggio wanted the scene set in a transcendent and otherworldly environment.
Caravaggio wanted to the scene to appear integrated with the chapel's architectural environment.
Caravaggio wanted to set the scene in a classical environment of the past.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which detail  indicates the presence of the divine in this composition?**

![Painting showing Christ, at right with a halo, pointing toward a group of men at a table in a dark tavern-like setting, with a beam of light highlighting the surprised expressions of the figures.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/e2f72f2355b8c6682ac4a140881ef55a.jpg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Caravaggio used light as an embodiment of a divine spiritual force that enters the room with Christ. 

**[Watch this video to review.](/humanities/monarchy-enlightenment/baroque-art1/baroque-italy/v/caravaggio-s-calling-of-st-matthew-c-1599-1600)**

choices.content: the light streaming in from the corner
the surprised expression on Matthew's face
the bare feet of Peter and Christ
the objects sitting on the table

assessment_items.question_content:
**How does Caravaggio's *The Calling of St. Matthew* differ from art of the High Renaissance?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Like artists of the High Renaissance, Baroque artists often painted religious subject matter.  So, that choice is one way in which Baroque art did not differ from Renaissance art.

Baroque artists such as Caravaggio rejected the classical idealism of the Renaissance. Baroque artists like Caravaggio showed figures that are more real than ideal.

**[Watch this video to review.](/humanities/monarchy-enlightenment/baroque-art1/baroque-italy/v/caravaggio-s-calling-of-st-matthew-c-1599-1600)**

choices.content: It suggests a moment that's been frozen in time.
It emphasizes everyday life and worldly experience.
It focuses more on the real, than on the ideal.
It focuses on religious subject matter.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which best describes the underlying meaning of this painting?**

![Painting showing Christ, at right with a halo, pointing toward a group of men at a table in a dark tavern-like setting, with a beam of light highlighting the surprised expressions of the figures.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/e2f72f2355b8c6682ac4a140881ef55a.jpg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Caravaggio captured the moment of Matthew's spiritual awakening and  transformation, when he gives up his life as a tax collector to follow Christ's call. 

**[Watch this video to review.](/humanities/monarchy-enlightenment/baroque-art1/baroque-italy/v/caravaggio-s-calling-of-st-matthew-c-1599-1600)**

choices.content: It's a painting about spiritual awakening.
It's a painting about guilt and remorse.
It's a painting about sinfulness and greed.
